Title: Tailoring the composition of novel wax esters in the seeds of transgenic Camelina sativa through systematic metabolic engineering

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter used only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core reductase for engineering seed wax esters in Camelina. Best deployed in combination with a compatible WS and tailored substrate-pool engineering; stacking with mouse WS plus medium-chain thioesterases increased WE accumulation strongly, whereas pairing with hydroxylase reduced yield.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces fatty acyl-CoA substrates to the corresponding fatty alcohols using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled de novo wax ester production in Camelina seeds and, with suitable WS partners, supported accumulation of tailored WE species; broad substrate use permitted incorporation of varied chain lengths, but hydroxylated substrates were used inefficiently.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic expression in Camelina; combinatorial co-expression with WS and substrate-modifying enzymes; HPLC-ELSD quantification of WEs; GC analysis of intact WEs and released fatty alcohol/acyl moieties; acyl-CoA profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Provides the fatty alcohol half of the target product directly.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds; plants grown at 23 °C day/18 °C night, 16-h photoperiod.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: M. hydrocarbonoclasticus WS; M. musculus WS; A. calcoaceticus WS; T. thermophila WS; Cuphea hookeriana Thio10; Umbellularia californica Thio12; Cuphea palustris Thio14; Claviceps purpurea CpFAH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states M. aquaeolei FAR accepts diverse C10-C20, unsaturated and substituted acyl-CoAs, but in planta performance depended strongly on the WS partner. Highest reported activity in prior work is with 18:1-CoA; fatty alcohol production from ricinoleoyl-CoA is reduced by ~75%, consistent with low WE yield in CpFAH lines.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fatty alcohol-forming branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High WE accumulation often coincided with reduced TAG percentage and variable seed weight, though no germination or obvious developmental defects were observed.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important enabling step but not shown as sole bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred from WE production context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific expression of MaFAR with different WS enzymes enabled non-native WE synthesis in Camelina seeds. 2. With mouse WS plus Thio10, it supported one of the highest WE accumulations observed (74.6 nmol/seed in T2). 3. Its broad substrate range allowed use of medium-chain pools, but hydroxylated substrates from CpFAH were incorporated poorly, limiting WE yield.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A productive WS for generating tailored, shorter-chain spermaceti-like WEs when combined with MaFAR and medium-chain thioesterases; favorable partner when substrate-pool remodeling is intended to shift WE composition.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from fatty acyl-CoA to a fatty alcohol to form a wax ester.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directed robust WE synthesis in Camelina and efficiently incorporated altered substrate pools, especially after co-expression of medium-chain thioesterases, producing broad C30-C42 WE profiles and shifts toward shorter WEs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic expression; HPLC-ELSD quantification of WEs; GC analysis of intact WEs and released acyl/alcohol moieties; comparison across MaMaWt, MaMa10, MaMa12, MaMa14, MaMaOH lines.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MaFAR; TtFARAT; Cuphea hookeriana Thio10; Umbellularia californica Thio12; Cuphea palustris Thio14; Claviceps purpurea CpFAH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with mouse WS, this WS more effectively converted remodeled substrate pools into altered WE composition. In MaMa14, likely major WE species shifted from 18:1/18:1 and 18:1/20:1 toward 14:0/18:1 and 14:0/20:1-containing WEs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal esterification branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some high-WE lines showed reduced TAG percentage and altered seed oil composition, though some lines retained or increased total oil per seed.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for substrate incorporation specificity; supported by differential response to altered acyl-CoA pools.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaWS with MaFAR produced high levels of WEs in Camelina seeds. 2. Unlike mouse WS, MaWS responded strongly to medium-chain substrate-pool engineering, shifting WE composition toward shorter chains. 3. MaWS-containing combinations generated broad C30-C42 distributions and enabled production of spermaceti-like WEs.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong choice for maximizing WE accumulation when paired with MaFAR and medium-chain thioesterases, especially Thio10 or Thio12, but less effective than MaWS for transferring remodeled substrate pools into diversified final WE composition.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from fatty acyl-CoA to a fatty alcohol to form a wax ester.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported substantial WE synthesis; MoMa10 reached 74.6 nmol/seed in T2, but final WE composition remained relatively specific (mainly C36/C38) despite strong remodeling of the acyl-CoA pool.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic expression; HPLC-ELSD WE quantification; GC compositional analysis; comparison of MoMaWt, MoMa10, MoMa12, MoMa14, MoMaOH lines; acyl-CoA profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MaFAR; Cuphea hookeriana Thio10; Umbellularia californica Thio12; Cuphea palustris Thio14; Claviceps purpurea CpFAH
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously reported to have broad specificity, in Camelina it preferentially yielded WEs with C18 acyl and C18/C20 alcohol moieties, mainly C38 and C36 species. Substantial substrate-pool remodeling by thioesterases was not efficiently reflected in WE composition.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal esterification branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High-accumulating MoMa10 lines had low TAG percentage, though some lines such as T3 MoMa14 combined high WEs with increased TAG per seed versus WT.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for product profile and WE accumulation response to substrate engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MmWS with MaFAR gave high WE accumulation in Camelina, especially with Thio10 and Thio12. 2. Despite altered acyl-CoA pools, MmWS maintained a specific C36/C38-dominant WE profile, indicating selective substrate use in planta. 3. Only Thio14 caused a notable shift toward shorter WEs in the MoMa background.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastid fatty acid synthesis / precursor supply for wax ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful substrate-pool engineering gene for increasing medium-chain precursors and boosting WE accumulation with compatible WS/FAR combinations, notably MmWS+MaFAR. Gene stacking should prioritize enzyme combinations able to actually incorporate released medium-chain acyl-CoAs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes medium-chain acyl-ACP, mainly 8:0- and 10:0-ACP, to release free fatty acids that enter the acyl-CoA pool after export/activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased medium-chain substrates and, with MmWS+MaFAR, produced the highest mean WE accumulation in the study (MoMa10, 74.6 nmol/seed); also shortened WE chain lengths in compatible backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression in Camelina; seed fatty acid profiling; acyl-CoA profiling; HPLC-ELSD WE quantification; GC WE composition analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its effect depended strongly on the WS present. In the MoMa background it greatly boosted WE accumulation; in MaMa background it did not improve total WE output despite modifying pools.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with altered TAG composition and in some lines reduced TAG percentage; no obvious germination or growth defects reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Free 8:0/10:0 fatty acids, then corresponding acyl-CoAs indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply engineering step, context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Thio10 remodeled the seed substrate pool toward medium-chain acyl species. 2. In the MmWS+MaFAR background it produced the highest WE accumulation observed among T2 lines. 3. Its benefit depended on downstream WS specificity, showing substrate engineering alone is insufficient.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastid fatty acid synthesis / precursor supply for wax ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to enrich 12:0-derived substrate pools and improve WE production with selected WS/FAR combinations; should be stacked with downstream enzymes proven to use these pools efficiently.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes mainly 12:0-ACP to release laurate for export and conversion to acyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased medium-chain fatty acids and supported elevated WE accumulation, especially in MoMa12 and MaMa12 backgrounds, with MaMa12 giving the highest MaWS-background T2 WE level (44.8 nmol/seed).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression; seed fatty acid profiling; acyl-CoA profiling; HPLC-ELSD WE quantification; GC analysis of WE chain-length distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like other thioesterases, its benefit depended on the downstream WS. It increased the medium-chain fraction of seed oil and acyl-CoAs but final WE composition shifts were background dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changed TAG composition and could accompany reduced TAG percentage in some high-WE backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply engineering step, context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Thio12 increased medium-chain precursor availability in Camelina seeds. 2. It supported elevated WE accumulation in both MaWS and MmWS backgrounds, with strong performance in MaMa12 and MoMa12 lines. 3. Final product outcome still depended on downstream WS/FAR specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Plastid fatty acid synthesis / precursor supply for wax ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for tailoring spermaceti-like shorter-chain WEs. Particularly effective with MaWS or TtFARAT backgrounds for shortening WE composition, and selected T3 lines combined high WEs with increased TAG per seed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes mainly 14:0-ACP to release myristate for export and conversion to acyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Raised 14:0 levels in seed oil, shifted WE composition toward shorter C30-C34/C32-C38 species depending on WS/FAR background, and in selected T3 MaMa14 and MoMa14 lines supported major increases in WE content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression; seed fatty acid profiling; acyl-CoA profiling; HPLC-ELSD WE quantification including T3 lines; GC WE composition analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MaFAR; MaWS; MmWS; TtFARAT; A. calcoaceticus WS; T. thermophila WS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Acyl-CoA LC-MS/MS profiling (Table S1); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In MaMa14, only a small increase in 14:0-CoA was detected despite strong compositional effects, interpreted as efficient incorporation into WEs by MaWS. In T3 MoMa14, both WEs and TAG per seed exceeded WT levels.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Often altered TAG composition and could lower TAG percentage, though some T3 lines showed improved combined oil and WE accumulation. No germination/developmental defect observed.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply engineering step for chain-length tailoring.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Thio14 was a powerful chain-length tailoring tool, increasing medium-chain fatty acids and shifting WE profiles shorter. 2. Its impact depended on the WS/FAR combination, strongly affecting MaMa14, MoMa14, AcMa14, TeMa14 and MaTe14 outputs. 3. Selected T3 lines with Thio14 achieved large WE gains while maintaining or increasing TAG per seed.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hydroxylated fatty acid production feeding wax ester biosynthesis indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates a route to introduce unusual hydroxy-acyl moieties into seed WEs, but current flux is low and causes oil penalties; future engineering should improve acyl exchange and pairing with FAR/WS activities that better accept ricinoleate-derived substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates oleate at the Δ12 position on phospholipid-linked substrate to form ricinoleic acid (12-hydroxyoctadec-cis-9-enoic acid).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled accumulation of hydroxylated fatty acids in Camelina seed TAG and limited incorporation into WEs, but greatly reduced total WE yield compared with thioesterase-based combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression; seed fatty acid GC analysis showing 18:1OH accumulation; HPLC-ELSD WE quantification; comparison of MaMaOH and MoMaOH compositions.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on phospholipid-esterified substrates, so hydroxylated fatty acids reach WE synthesis only via endogenous acyl exchange from PC to acyl-CoA. MaMaOH incorporated more Δ12-OH-C18:1 into WEs than MoMaOH, showing strong dependence on downstream enzyme compatibility.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lines expressing CpFAH were compromised in oil synthesis (24%-26% TAG) and showed low WE accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Ricinoleic acid (12-OH-18:1) on phospholipid, then indirectly hydroxylated acyl-CoA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; precursor supply step with major downstream constraints.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum / membrane phospholipid-associated inferred from substrate context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CpFAH created hydroxylated fatty acid substrates in Camelina seeds. 2. These substrates were incorporated into WEs only modestly, with strong dependence on WS/FAR combination. 3. CpFAH expression reduced oil accumulation, revealing a tradeoff for unusual-substrate engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative WS for diversifying product profile rather than maximizing yield. Can be used with MaFAR and Thio14 to generate distinct C34-C38-rich WEs, but yield is moderate.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from fatty acyl-CoA to a fatty alcohol to form a wax ester; bifunctional enzyme also has DGAT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced moderate WE accumulation in Camelina with a distinct chain-length profile when co-expressed with MaFAR and Thio14.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression in AcMa14 lines; HPLC-ELSD quantification; GC compositional profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a broad-specificity bifunctional WS that also functions as DGAT. In AcMa14, WEs showed a C34-C38 distribution with C38 about 39%.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal esterification branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Moderate WE yield; bifunctionality suggests possible competition with TAG assembly, though this was not dissected directly.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcWS enabled WE production in Camelina when combined with MaFAR and Thio14. 2. It generated a distinct C34-C38 WE composition, different from MaWS and MmWS outputs. 3. It is useful for product diversification but not the top-yielding WS in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative WS for generating specific medium-chain-enriched WE compositions with MaFAR plus Thio14. Suitable for tuning acyl moiety composition rather than maximizing total WE yield.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from fatty acyl-CoA to a fatty alcohol to form a wax ester.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported moderate WE production and produced a distinct C32-C38 profile, incorporating greater amounts of shorter saturated acyl moieties than AcWS in the same substrate background.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression in TeMa14 lines; HPLC-ELSD quantification; GC compositional profiling of intact WEs and moieties.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In TeMa14, WE distribution ranged from C32 to C38 with C34 about 30%. Compared with AcWS, it incorporated more short saturated acyl moieties while maintaining a similar alcohol profile.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal esterification branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Moderate WE yield; not the highest-accumulating WS tested.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TtWS produced WEs in Camelina when paired with MaFAR and Thio14. 2. It generated a distinct C32-C38 profile and favored shorter saturated acyl incorporation relative to AcWS. 3. It provides an additional tool for fine-tuning WE composition.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific conlinin1 promoter (pCnl); constitutive CsVMV promoter only for DsRed selection marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative fatty alcohol-producing activity for generating shorter-chain WEs with MaWS plus Thio14, but low yield limits immediate deployment. Could be useful when shorter alcohol moieties are specifically desired.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional fatty acyl reductase/acyltransferase activity used here primarily as FAR, reducing fatty acyl substrates to fatty alcohols; in native context may also catalyze wax ester formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With MaWS, produced only low WE accumulation but shifted product spectrum toward shorter WEs by more efficient conversion of short-chain acyl-CoAs to alcohols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific transgenic co-expression in MaTeWt and MaTe14 lines; HPLC-ELSD quantification; GC WE composition and moiety analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Provides the fatty alcohol half of the target product directly.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed-specific expression in developing seeds under controlled growth conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed seed-specifically in Camelina seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to it as a bifunctional FAR gene from T. thermophile/thermophila. In MaTe14 it generated predominantly C30-C36 WEs and higher C12+C14 alcohol content than MaFAR, but total WEs remained low.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fatty alcohol-forming branch of seed WE assembly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very low WE yield in MaTe backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fatty alcohols (and potentially wax esters in native bifunctional context)
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated/seed lipid biosynthetic compartment inferred in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TtFARAT functioned in Camelina with MaWS to produce WEs, confirming in planta activity. 2. It favored shorter-chain alcohol formation, leading to shorter WEs in MaTe14. 3. Despite compositional benefits, it gave the lowest WE yields among the tested reductase systems.
